name: Ben [Last Name], PE
leadBio: Timber Ridge Engineering & Design (TREAD) is a McMinnville-based structural engineering and home design firm. Ben brings a hands-on, collaborative approach to every project — delivering designs that are structurally sound, cost-effective, and contractor-ready.
bio1: Ben started TREAD because he saw a gap: homeowners and contractors struggling to coordinate between separate engineering and design firms, leading to delays, miscommunication, and drawings that didn't reflect real-world construction constraints. By combining both disciplines under one roof, TREAD delivers a smoother process from the first sketch to the final stamp.
bio2: Every project gets Ben's direct attention — no junior staff handoffs, no losing details in translation.
process1Desc: Every project starts with a conversation about your goals, budget, and timeline — before any drawings are made.
process2Desc: Every detail on the drawings reflects how things are actually built in the field — materials available, trades involved, permit requirements.
process3Desc: Ben stays reachable throughout construction. When the contractor has a question, you get a real answer fast — not a week later.
value1Desc: Designs are made to be built, not just drawn. Ben considers material availability, construction sequencing, and contractor workflow in every document.
value2Desc: Good engineering finds the efficient solution — not the over-engineered one. The goal is a building that performs well and a budget that holds.
value3Desc: Ben works with homeowners, general contractors, and architects as a team member, not just a stamping service. Communication is part of the job.
